name: database-migration-helper
description: Creates database migration files following project conventions for Prisma, Sequelize, Alembic, Knex, TypeORM, and other ORMs. Use when adding tables, modifying schemas, or when user mentions database changes.
capabilities: Detects ORM framework from project files
Creates migration files with proper naming conventions
Generates up and down or rollback methods
Includes indexes, foreign keys, and constraints
Follows project-specific patterns and directory structure
author: CrazyDubya
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing migration templates and guidance for 10+ ORM frameworks. Static findings are false positives - all 109 external_commands detections are documentation examples of ORM CLI commands (not actual execution), 31 weak_crypto detections are standard database identifiers (UUID, gen_random_uuid) not cryptographic operations, and 3 reconnaissance detections are legitimate ORM detection documentation. No executable code, network operations, or sensitive data access.
valueStatement: Database migrations are tedious to write manually and easy to get wrong. This skill generates properly formatted migration files for any ORM with up and down methods, follows your project conventions, and includes rollback support.
limitations: Does not execute migrations on databases
Does not validate migration logic against existing schema
Does not generate data transformation scripts
Requires user to review and run generated migrations

useCases.title: Add new tables and columns
useCases.description: Generate migrations for new database tables with proper types, constraints, and relationships

useCases.title: Modify existing schemas
useCases.description: Create migrations that alter columns, add indexes, or update table structures safely

useCases.title: Standardize migration patterns
useCases.description: Ensure consistent naming conventions and rollback support across multiple projects

promptTemplates.title: Create users table
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a migration to add a users table with id (uuid), email (unique, not null), name, created_at, and updated_at fields

promptTemplates.title: Add column to table
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a status column to the orders table with a default value of pending and a foreign key reference to users

promptTemplates.title: Create index
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a migration that adds a unique index on the email column in the users table

promptTemplates.title: Rollback migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a migration that drops the products table and its indexes to roll back the previous change

bestPractices: Always test migrations on development database before production
Include both forward and rollback methods for safe reversibility
Use descriptive names that explain what the migration does
antiPatterns: Combining multiple unrelated schema changes in one migration
Using vague names like migration1 or update
Skipping rollback/down methods for complex migrations

faq.question: Which ORM frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Supports Prisma, Sequelize, Knex, TypeORM, Alembic (Python), Rails, Django, Flyway, and Liquibase.

faq.question: Does this execute migrations?
faq.answer: No, it only generates migration files. You must review and run them using your ORM CLI tools.

faq.question: How are migrations named?
faq.answer: Uses timestamp-based naming (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description) or sequential numbering following project conventions.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill only generates migration file content. It does not access your database or execute any commands.

faq.question: What if my project uses a custom ORM?
faq.answer: Provide your existing migration files and patterns, and the skill will follow your project's conventions.

faq.question: How is this different from ORM CLI tools?
faq.answer: ORM CLI tools generate basic migrations. This skill helps create comprehensive migrations with proper constraints, indexes, and rollback support.
